title: Competency Portfolio and First-6mo Tooling Roadmap

text:
Overview:

This document defines the competency portfolio approach used at Redwood for the first six months of employment, and the practical tooling and access roadmap that ensures new hires can execute against both onboarding deliverables and early career growth goals. It is intended for new hires, hiring managers, people-ops partners, and engineering managers who own role activation and early-career development.

Purpose:
- Reduce time-to-impact by making role responsibilities, success criteria, and required tools explicit for months 0–6.
- Tie access and task milestones to measurable competency checkpoints that map to the company role ladder.
- Provide reusable templates for managers to run consistent, equitable onboarding and early development cycles.

Scope and audience:
- Applies to full-time engineering, product, and core platform hires. Individual contributor (IC) and manager ...

title: New-Joiner Systems & Career Activation Manual
preview:
Overview
--------
This manual consolidates the practical systems, access steps, and role-aligned activation checkpoints every new Redwood engineer or product hire needs to get productive quickly. It complements the People Ops orientation and the team-specific 30/90 plans by focusing on the *systems* and *career acti...
text:
Overview
--------
This manual consolidates the practical systems, access steps, and role-aligned activation checkpoints every new Redwood engineer or product hire needs to get productive quickly. It complements the People Ops orientation and the team-specific 30/90 plans by focusing on the *systems* and *career activation points* — accounts, permissions, tooling, and the milestones that unlock career progress.

Use this as a single-source checklist during the first 6 months; owners and tooling contacts are listed for each item.

Scope
-----
- Audience: all new full-time and contractor hires across Engineering, Product, and Platform teams.
- Exclusions: individual contributor interview/hiring guides, team-specific onboarding curricula (those live on team wikis).
- Outcomes: verified access to core systems, completed security & compliance steps, and alignment on career ladder milestones...

title: Growth Micro-Paths and Skill Sprint Handbook
preview:
Overview
This handbook defines Redwood's Growth Micro-Paths and Skill Sprints program: a lightweight, time-boxed set of learning and delivery activities (1–6 weeks) that let contributors (ICs and managers) gain targeted skills, demonstrate growth toward a ladder milestone, and produce a small, shippable artifact tha...
text:
Overview
This handbook defines Redwood's Growth Micro-Paths and Skill Sprints program: a lightweight, time-boxed set of learning and delivery activities (1–6 weeks) that let contributors (ICs and managers) gain targeted skills, demonstrate growth toward a ladder milestone, and produce a small, shippable artifact that benefits a team or product.

Why we run micro-paths
- Fast, measurable skill development aligned to ladder competencies.
- Low-friction way to pilot cross-team rotations and internal mobility.
- Provides input signals to performance calibration (work samples) without long-term role changes.

Scope and audience
- Audience: Individual contributors, new managers, hiring managers, people-ops partners, and engineering leads.
- Scope: Technical and non-technical micro-paths (e.g., inference performance tuning, product analytics, onboarding docs, customer playbooks, security rev...

title: Career Neighborhoods and Benefits Playbook — Neighborhoods, Ladders, and Access
preview:
Purpose

This playbook introduces Redwood's career neighborhoods model, the expectations that power our leveling and promotion decisions, a high-level benefits snapshot tied to role families, and the internal tools and access patterns managers and new hires need to move quickly. The goal is to reduce ambiguity: give...
text:
Purpose

This playbook introduces Redwood's career neighborhoods model, the expectations that power our leveling and promotion decisions, a high-level benefits snapshot tied to role families, and the internal tools and access patterns managers and new hires need to move quickly. The goal is to reduce ambiguity: give managers and contributors a single reference for role scope, compensation components, promotion mechanics, and the operational steps for hiring/onboarding and role changes.

Scope and audience

- Audience: managers, ICs, people-ops partners, hiring managers.
- Scope: Career family organization, leveling rubric and example expectations, benefits summary, hiring & onboarding checklists, internal tools access matrix, promotion and rotation procedures.

Principles and design goals

1. Role neighborhoods make tradeoffs explicit: group adjacent specialties so lateral moves and r...

title: Benefits and Life-Transition Playbook: Hiring, Ladders, and Tools
preview:
Overview

Purpose:
This playbook explains how Redwood supports contributors through major life transitions (new hire, parenthood, relocation, medical leave, sabbatical, retirement) and ties those policies into hiring timelines, role and career ladder expectations, and the internal tools that make transitions smooth....
text:
Overview

Purpose:
This playbook explains how Redwood supports contributors through major life transitions (new hire, parenthood, relocation, medical leave, sabbatical, retirement) and ties those policies into hiring timelines, role and career ladder expectations, and the internal tools that make transitions smooth. The audience is hiring managers, new joiners, people-ops partners, and people engineering partners who own role design or tool access.

Scope:
Applies to all full-time and fixed-term employees globally. Contractors should consult their engagement contract and People Ops for region-specific exceptions.

Quick reference (one-line summaries):
- Hiring SLA target: 45 days average time-to-offer; expedite pathways for mission-critical roles.
- Onboarding completion goal: 30 days for systems + 90 days to reach expected role impact.
- Benefits changes for life events: notify Peopl...

title: Role Clarity Playbook for Cross-Functional Contributors
preview:
Summary:

This playbook defines how Redwood documents, assesses, and evolves roles that span multiple domains (for example: product-engineering, design-research, platform-customer-success). Its goal is to reduce ambiguity for individual contributors and managers, accelerate onboarding, and create consistent promotio...
text:
Summary:

This playbook defines how Redwood documents, assesses, and evolves roles that span multiple domains (for example: product-engineering, design-research, platform-customer-success). Its goal is to reduce ambiguity for individual contributors and managers, accelerate onboarding, and create consistent promotion and mobility paths for cross-functional contributors.

Purpose:
- Provide a single, repeatable process for creating and updating cross-functional role definitions.
- Describe leveling expectations and success criteria that are comparable across tracks.
- Offer a minimally prescriptive template teams can use to document responsibilities, decision rights, and interfaces.

Scope:
- Applies to individual contributor and manager roles that regularly operate across >1 core functional area (engineering, product, design, research, SRE, customer-success).
- Not intended for contra...

text:
Overview:

This handbook bundles the practical tooling and milestone calendar for new joiners (ICs and first-time managers) and the Role Sprint process we use to accelerate early-career growth. It is intended to be a single reference for people-ops partners, hiring managers, mentors, and new teammates during their first 120 days and beyond.

Audience:
- New individual contributors (IC1–IC3) and first-time managers.
- Hiring managers and onboarding buddies.
- People-ops and engineering managers who run Role Sprints.

Scope:
- Tool access and minimal config for day‑one productivity.
- A repeatable 6-week Role Sprint format for skill acceleration.
- An aligned set of role-ladder checkpoints and promotion-ready evidence.
- Benefits and perks orientation checklist relevant to first 120 days.

Ownership and SLAs:
- Owner: People Ops - New Hire Experience (people-ops@redwood.ai).

Summary:
This document describes Redwood's workplace architecture and career ecosystem: the company-wide principles that guide how we work, the formal role taxonomy and promotion criteria, a high-level hiring and onboarding flow, benefits highlights relevant to career decisions, and the internal tools & access patterns that enable fast, secure ramp. - Purpose: Provide a single source of truth for how roles are defined, how career progress works across IC and manager tracks, and the operational details that support new-hire success.
- Scope: Applies to all full-time employees and contractors who are on a multi-month hiring/workflow. Does not replace team-level comp bands or role-specific scorecards which are maintained by hiring managers.

Overview

Purpose: This playbook documents how Redwood Inference activates, verifies, and maintains employee benefits eligibility across role changes, onboarding events, leaves, and internal transfers. It is intended for people-ops partners, hiring managers, and enrollment specialists.

title: First Week Systems & Badge Onboarding Playbook
preview:
Overview

This playbook describes the recommended, low-friction process for new hires to obtain systems access, physical badge entry, and the core toolchain needed to be productive in their first week. It is intended for people-ops partners, hiring managers, IT/staff engineers, and hiring-team buddies who coordinate...
text:
Overview

This playbook describes the recommended, low-friction process for new hires to obtain systems access, physical badge entry, and the core toolchain needed to be productive in their first week. It is intended for people-ops partners, hiring managers, IT/staff engineers, and hiring-team buddies who coordinate access. The emphasis is on automated flows, clear owners, and fallback runbooks for common failures.

Intended outcomes (by the end of week 1)
- Employee has building badge and primary laptop configured.
- Access to core cloud projects, code repositories, and primary communication tools (Slack, email, calendar).
- Access requests are auditable and issued with minimum-privilege roles.
- New hire is able to run the local dev quickstart and join at least one team-sync.

Owners & escalation
- Primary owner: people-ops (badge logistics, orientation scheduling)
- Systems owner: ...

...
text:
Summary

This page documents a lightweight, experimental set of guidelines for beanbag seating and ambient noise considerations in Redwood Inference shared spaces (cafeteria, lounge, and overflow areas). The goal is pragmatic: improve employee comfort and informal collaboration while keeping operational burden low.

Scope

- Applies to: cafeteria, adjacent lounge, satellite huddle areas on main campus and leased offices.
- Does NOT apply to: focus rooms, phone booths, secure rooms, or private customer meeting rooms.

Guiding principles

1. Respect for teammates: conserve shared spaces for implicit consent — assume people nearby prefer moderate noise levels.
2. Minimal ops: prefer low-maintenance furniture and sensors; avoid complex approvals for small purchases.
3. Data-informed: measure and iterate; use a simple decibel sampling process before large rollouts.

Furniture guidelines (b...
